title: Puerto Banus Dolphin Watching Catamaran Hire Costs and Tips 2026
html:
Dolphin watching catamaran hire in Puerto Banus typically costs from €60 per person for a shared tour up to €4,500 for a private full-day charter. Departing from the marina, these trips explore the Mediterranean Sea. BoatHire24 connects you with verified operators offering this experience.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus

Most dolphin watching catamarans depart from Puerto Banus marina, a purpose-built harbour west of Marbella. You also find departures from Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo de Marbella) and from Estepona harbour to the west. Some operators pick up from Cabopino Marina near Artola dunes. The boats head south-west toward the Strait of Gibraltar, passing Cabo de Marbella and the waters off San Pedro de Alcantara. Bottlenose and striped dolphins are frequent sightings here.

Which boat to rent for dolphin watching

  1. Shared catamaran tour, best for solo travellers or couples, up to 12 guests, from €60 per person.
  2. Small motorboat, ideal for a family or small group, 6, 8 guests, half-day from €350.
  3. Private catamaran, spacious for groups up to 12, includes crew, full-day from €1,200.
  4. Crewed luxury yacht, such as an Azimut or Sunseeker, for up to 10 guests, full-day from €3,500.
  5. Sailing yacht, classic experience for up to 8 guests, half-day from €700. Visit the Sanyati charter page to see a 51-foot Leopard example.

How much does it cost to rent a catamaran in Puerto Banus?

Prices range from €60 per person for a shared trip to €4,500 for a private full-day catamaran.

  • Shared dolphin tour (2, 3 hours): €60, €90 per person.
  • Small motorboat hire half-day (4 hours): €350, €600.
  • Private catamaran full-day (8 hours): €1,200, €2,500.
  • Crewed luxury yacht full-day (up to 12 guests): €3,000, €4,500.

All prices in EUR; currency is euro. To compare options, explore yacht charter in Puerto Banus on BoatHire24.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Puerto Banus?

For a catamaran with a skipper, no licence is required; bareboat hire requires a valid ICC or equivalent.

Spanish regulations require anyone renting a bareboat without crew to hold an International Certificate of Competence (ICC) or a national licence recognised by Spain. For dolphin watching trips, virtually all operators provide a skipper, so no licence is needed. The operator confirms exact requirements during booking.

On early morning trips dolphins often surface close to the marina entrance, within 15 minutes of departing, giving you excellent photo opportunities without losing sailing time.

What is the best season for dolphin watching in Puerto Banus?

The peak season runs June to September, when sea conditions are calm and sightings reliable. The local Poniente wind (west wind) can kick up in the afternoon, so morning departures are recommended. May and October also offer good sightings with fewer crowds. In 2026, anticipate high demand for summer weekends.

At a glance

  • Price band: €60, €4,500
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: 2, 8 hours
  • Capacity: up to 12 guests on catamarans
  • Currency: EUR (euro)

Questions about boat rental in Puerto Banus

Can I see dolphins every time I go out?

Sightings are frequent but not guaranteed; dolphin pods live in the area year-round, with success rates above 80 per cent in summer.

What time of day is best for dolphin watching?

Early morning trips (9, 10 am) offer the calmest seas and best light, often reaching the dolphin grounds within 20 minutes.

Are children allowed on dolphin watching trips?

Yes, most catamarans welcome children, and life jackets are provided; some tours offer a reduced child rate.

How far offshore do the boats go?

Trips typically cover 5 to 15 nautical miles from the coast, staying within sight of land.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Puerto Banus or list your own. To become a host and offer your vessel for dolphin watching, become a host on the platform.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Puerto Banus and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
